rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order: 1) Issuing A Writ Of Possession To Remove Archibald Cunningham From The Property Located At 1489 Mcallister St, Sf, Ca 2) Respondent Is Order To Stay Away From 1489 Mcallister St, Sf, Ca / Dec Of Rick Marquis; Dec Of J. Scott Mckay; Dec Of Kevin Singer
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 6, 2014, line 1. -OTHER KEVIN SINGER'S Motion For Order: 1) Issuing A Writ Of Possession To Remove Archibald Cunningham From The Property Located At 1489 Mcallister St, Sf, Ca 2) Respondent Is Order To Stay Away From 1489 Mcallister St, Sf, Ca / Dec Of Rick Marquis; Dec Of J. Scott Mckay; Dec Of Kevin Singer. Receiver's Motion for a Writ of Possession. Hearing required to address whether remedy of writ of possession is proper for Defendant's alleged non-compliance with the injunction order. Defendant's late-filed opposition and disguised sur-reply in the form of Motion to Strike filed on April 28, 2014 are stricken. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PARK LANE ASSOCIATES, LP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. JOSEPH ALIOTO, AN INDIVIDUAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Vacate Dismissal And Enter Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 6, 2014, line 4. -Plaintiff'S Motion To Vacate Dismissal And Enter Judgment. Plaintiff's Motion to Vacate Dismissal and Enter Judgment is GRANTED. Defendant released all claims arising from building's extensive renovation. "This release extends to all past, current, and future construction activity that occurs in or around the building, the Premises, and/or other units in The Park Lane." Defendants' assumption that "other units" did not include unit directly above defendants is not a mistake of fact. Defendant also failed to show that any construction was concealed from defendants. Defendant failed to comply with paragraph 8(ii) of the settlement agreement. Remedy, if any, of paying rent into an escrow pending resolution of habitability claims is not available when such claims have been expressly released. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SKRSFO HOSPITALITY, LLC, DOING BUSINESS AS "CW VS. MAURICE MARTINEZ et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 6, 2014, line 6. -DEFENDANT MAURICE MARTINEZ'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is sustained without leave to amend. "3 Day Notice" served on March 20, 2014 cannot support an unlawful detainer filed on March 3, 2014. Plaintiff and its counsel are ordered to comply with SFRO 2.6B in future. = (501/REQ)
